title: filipendula multijuga subsp. koreana
intro: Filipendula multijuga subsp. koreana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly known for its showy white or pink flowers and medicinal properties. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Korean and Chinese medicine, for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Filipendula multijuga subsp. koreana include reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its dried roots and flowers are also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
